programs.programName: Medicaid for the Aged, Blind, or Disabled (ABD)

verified.notes: Age threshold is 65+ for seniors in ABD Medicaid. Income limits are based on SSI benefit rates (100% FBR): $994 individual, $1,491 couple for 2026. Asset limits not published in retrieved sources; Ohio ABD is described as 'income and resource based' but specific countable asset caps are not provided in available search results. Phone number 800-324-8680 is listed for Ohio Medicaid enrollment.

programs.notes: Age threshold is 65+ for seniors in ABD Medicaid. Income limits are based on SSI benefit rates (100% FBR): $994 individual, $1,491 couple for 2026. Asset limits not published in retrieved sources; Ohio ABD is described as 'income and resource based' but specific countable asset caps are not provided in available search results. Phone number 800-324-8680 is listed for Ohio Medicaid enrollment.

verified.notes: Age 60 is the minimum for seniors; physically disabled persons aged 60-64 also qualify. Income limit of $2,901/month (2025 rate, adjusted annually in January) applies to individual applicants regardless of marital status. For married couples where both are applicants, each spouse is evaluated individually at $2,901/month; non-applicant spouse income allowance varies based on shelter/utility costs but cannot exceed $3,948/month total. Asset limits are from 2024 data; current 2026 limits not found in search results. Phone number is toll-free for voicemail requests.

programs.notes: Age 60 is the minimum for seniors; physically disabled persons aged 60-64 also qualify. programs.programName: Ohio Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)

verified.notes: No income or asset limits found in sources; uses institutional Medicaid financial eligibility standards if seeking Medicaid assistance. Program active and expanding as of 2026.

programs.notes: No income or asset limits found in sources; uses institutional Medicaid financial eligibility standards if seeking Medicaid assistance. Program active and expanding as of 2026.

verified.notes: Multiple programs (QMB, SLMB, QI-1) with different income limits; no single value and no 2026 Ohio official source found for income. Assets consistent across sources for Ohio.

programs.notes: Multiple programs (QMB, SLMB, QI-1) with different income limits; no single value and no 2026 Ohio official source found for income. Assets consistent across sources for Ohio.

verified.notes: No authoritative data found on senior-specific age threshold, countable assets, or phone in search results; SNAP generally has no asset test for most households and eligibility is income-based with gross/net limits varying by household size

programs.notes: No authoritative data found on senior-specific age threshold, countable assets, or phone in search results; SNAP generally has no asset test for most households and eligibility is income-based with gross/net limits varying by household size

verified.notes: HEAP has no age requirement; program serves all eligible low-income households regardless of age. Income limits shown are for July 2025–May 2026 period (175% FPG for households ≤8 members). No asset test found in official sources. Program administration transferred to Ohio Department of Job and Family Services effective April 6, 2026. Current application deadline is May 31, 2026.

verified.notes: Program transitioning to Ohio Dept of Job and Family Services as of April 6, 2026; no statewide phone or asset limits found; priority for seniors over 60 but no age minimum

programs.programName: Ohio Senior Health Insurance Information Program (OSHIIP)

verified.notes: OSHIIP is a free counseling program for Medicare beneficiaries with no age, income, or asset eligibility requirements

verified.notes: NFCSP has no financial requirements (income/assets). Age 60+ applies to senior caregiving pathway. Phone number is Ohio-specific Area Agency on Aging connector line per official Ohio Department of Aging source. Program remains active as of 2026.

programs.notes: NFCSP has no financial requirements (income/assets). Age 60+ applies to senior caregiving pathway. Phone number is Ohio-specific Area Agency on Aging connector line per official Ohio Department of Aging source. Program remains active as of 2026.

verified.notes: No statewide phone or specific income/assets limits found; contact local providers like VANTAGE Aging for eligibility details. Program active as of 2026 per state sources.

programs.programName: Legal Assistance for Seniors (via Ohio Legal Services)

verified.notes: Ohio Legal Services refers to statewide legal aid network; primary senior program is Ohio's Legal Helpline via Pro Seniors (age 60+, no income/assets test)
